kubejs.ftbquests.quest.macera_basi.welco: Welcome to your new peaceful life! Balik is a cozy life-sim modpack about fishing, farming, cooking, and exploring a world full of seasons and secrets. Cast a line by the river, grow crops in your garden, cook warm meals, and build your dream homestead. Open your Quest Book to guide your journey.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.macera_basi.seaso: Serene Seasons alters crop fertility and temperature over time. Crops only grow in their fertile seasons, and winters can be freezing! Craft a Calendar to keep track of time and plan your harvests. A Season Sensor can also emit a redstone signal when a chosen season arrives, great for automating greenhouses.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.macera_basi.sol_c: Spice of Life (Carrot Edition) rewards you for eating unique foods. Eating new kinds of food grants permanent extra hearts! Collect and eat different foods to increase your max health. Start by gathering these basic foods.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.macera_basi.backp: Sophisticated Backpacks are highly customizable, upgradable backpack items that you can wear or place. Craft a basic Leather Backpack to ease your inventory management during exploration.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.macera_basi.sleep: Comforts sleeping bags allow you to sleep through the night without resetting your spawn point. Perfect for long journeys across the world!
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.macera_basi.compa: Nature's Compass and Explorer's Compass are vital tools for finding biomes and structures in this vast world. Craft either compass to help guide your exploration.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.macera_basi.wayst: Waystones let you teleport between any waystones you have activated, for a small emerald fee. Craft a Waystone, place it at your base, and activate it by right-clicking. Carry a Return Scroll to warp home from anywhere.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.macera_basi.marke: Farming for Blockheads adds a Market where you can trade emeralds for seeds, saplings, and other farming supplies. Once you understand the seasons, craft a Market and place it to browse all the seeds the modpack has to offer. Plan your crops according to the current season!
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.ciftcilik_ve_gast: A Chef's First Tool
Knives from Farmer's Delight are crucial for clean harvesting. Use them to get straw from wild grass, and to get clean meat cuts from animals. Craft a Flint Knife to start.
The Heart of the Kitchen
The Cooking Pot is the core of Farmer's Delight. Place it on a Stove or Campfire to prepare nourishing meals with lasting effects. Master the pot, master survival.
Bakery Apprentice
Let's Do Bakery introduces pastries, bread, and cakes. Place dough on a Tray and bake it in the oven. Bread making has never been this rewarding!
The Art of Winemaking
Let's Do Vinery introduces grape cultivation and winemaking. Collect Red and White Grapes, plant them on vines, and press them into juice. A journey from vineyard to bottle begins.
The Grand Feast
Prepare a full Roast Chicken Feast. Feasts are blocks that can be placed down and eaten multiple times by players, providing massive nourishment and status effects. A true master chef's achievement!
Automated Irrigation
Let's Do: Farm & Charm adds a Water Sprinkler that hydrates and accelerates nearby crops. Place it above a water source and watch your fields grow faster, even out of season with the right setup.
Tea Time
Farmer's Respite brings tea and coffee culture. Craft a Kettle, brew it on a heat source, and enjoy Green Tea for gentle, long-lasting effects. Strong and Long brews last even longer.
A Candlelight Dinner

Let's Do: Candlelight elevates dining to an art form. Craft a Hearth, lay a fine table, and prepare Beef Wellington or Salmon on White Wine. Complete the experience with a Chef's outfit — Hat, Jacket, Pants, and Boots.
Corn Delight
Corn Delight adds tall corn crops and a harvest of tasty recipes. Plant Corn Seeds, grow them into towering stalks, and craft comforting Cornbread or a warm Bowl of Corn Soup.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.hayvancilik_refor: DragN's Livestock Overhaul introduces genders to animals! Animals now require both a male and female to mate. Milk and wool are also gender-restricted. Craft a Gender Test Strip to easily identify an animal's gender if you are unsure.
Horses' manes and tails grow over time. Use Mane Scissors and Tail Scissors to groom them, change their hairstyles, and maintain their speed and performance.
Use color-coded Brand Tags to identify your animals. This helps manage breeding programs, dairy cattle, or sheep lineages in your farm.
Process your livestock meat into thin strips and dry them into Pork Jerky or Beef Strips. You can also boil eggs to make delicious egg salads. High nutrition and long shelf life!
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.f: Perhaps the most peaceful moment of your new life is watching your bobber float on the gentle water. Aquaculture replaces weak old fishing rods with durable, upgradable metal ones. Craft an Iron Fishing Rod to start your journey. Listen to the music of the rivers.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.t: A true fisherman never leaves their gear in disarray. The Tackle Box is your best companion to store rods, hooks, lines, and baits in one convenient place. Stay organized and focus on the catch!

With the Tide system, fish now move according to seasons, time of day, and weather conditions. Being in the right place at the right time is the angler's greatest virtue. Catch a Largemouth Bass to prove your skill.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.s: Scent of Fish in the Kitchen
Don't let your fresh catch go to waste. Use a chef's knife on a Farmer's Delight cutting board to slice your fish. These fresh slices will be the foundation of your gourmet dishes.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.c: When celestial bodies align and waters glow with mystic starlight, cosmic fish like the Neptune Koi ascend. Catching this legendary fish requires patience, perfect timing, and your best rod. Its cosmic scales hold great energy.
Set up a passive income by the shore with Crabber's Delight. Place a Crab Trap baited with fish into the water. Over time, crabs, clams, and shrimp will find their way inside while you rest.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.o: Turn guardians and squid into delicious meals. Cook up a bowl of hot Guardian Soup or roll some fresh Fugu Roll from pufferfish to offer your guests an unforgettable feast.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.f: The Fisherman's Trap catches fish in the background while you tend to your crops or explore caves. Place it in water, bait it, and return periodically to collect your bounty. Practical and efficient!
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.d: Time to leave the surface and explore the true kingdom beneath the waves. The Diving Helmet lets you breathe underwater and clears your vision in murky depths. The abyss awaits.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.muhendislik_ve_ot: Create introduces mechanical engineering and kinetic stress. To generate rotational force, craft a Water Wheel, some Shafts, and Cogwheels to transmit rotational energy.
The Mechanical Press squeezes ingots into metal sheets. Place items under it on a depot or belt to press them automatically. Crucial for advanced crafting recipes.
The Mechanical Mixer and Basin mix materials under rotational force. Some recipes require heat, which can be supplied by placing a Blaze Burner beneath the Basin.
Integrate Create machinery with Farmer's Delight using Slice & Dice or Create Central Kitchen. Automate food slicing or soup cooking to supply your colony or spice of life goals effortlessly!

Slice & Dice adds a Slicer that automates Farmer's Delight cutting-board recipes, and a Sprinkler driven by Create's rotation. Hook a Slicer to a belt and a depot to mass-produce sliced ingredients without lifting a finger.

Create Central Kitchen integrates Farmer's Delight cooking with Create: a Blaze Stove heats multiple pots at once and automated recipes pour out syrup, tomato sauce, and more. Grab the Cooking Guide to learn the recipes and start your factory kitchen.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.kesif_ve_kolonile: This world is full of wild beasts and birds. Craft the Animal Dictionary from Alex's Mobs to learn taming methods, drops, and ecosystem interactions for all creatures.
Alex's Caves introduces 5 subterranean biomes filled with magnetic forces, toxic waste, prehistoric dinosaurs, bioluminescent oceans, and dark hollows. Locate one using a Cave Tablet.

MineColonies lets you build a thriving city of citizens. Start by crafting a Supply Camp Deployer and right-clicking it on a flat, clear 16x17 area (no flowers, grass, or holes). Inside the deployed camp's rack you'll find the precious Build Tool and a Town Hall block — the two items every colony is founded on.

Hold the Build Tool, right-click the ground, and select the Town Hall to preview and place it. Then right-click the placed Town Hall block and choose Create Colony. Sign the Settlement Covenant and your first citizens will arrive shortly!

Citizens cannot construct buildings on their own. Build a Builder's Hut, place it with the Build Tool, assign a Builder, then open its GUI and pick Build Options to start construction. Keep the builder's chest supplied to grow your town.
Citizens get hungry and stop working if not fed. Establish a Farm Hut or Fishery Hut so your colonists can gather and produce food automatically. A Farmer also needs a Field block assigned nearby.
As your colony grows, barbarians and raiders will attack during the night. Build Guard Towers and recruit guards to defend your citizens!

Wildernature adds a Bounty Board where you can accept hunting contracts to track down wild animals for rewards. Craft a Bounty Board, take a Common Contract, and start earning loot bags and trophies from the wilderness.
Goblin Traders spawn deep in the caves and offer rare trades for emeralds. Some goblins even sell vein-mining tools. Explore the caves below, find a Goblin Trader, and trade for something shiny!
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.icecekler_ve_egle: Brewin' & Chewin' introduces the Keg, which ferments drinks and ages cheese. Place a Keg, fill it, and let time work its magic to brew Beer, Mead, Rice Wine, or Kombucha — each with unique effects.

Let's Do: Brewery adds a full brewing line. Build a Wooden Brewing Station, grow Hops, and craft mugs of Beer, Pretzels, and Sausage to host a proper brewfest. Don't drink too much, or the Breathalyzer will give you away!
Age a Flaxen Cheese Wheel in the Keg to turn it into a sharp, sliceable delicacy. Combine it with bread, pasta, or use it in Cheesy Pasta and Fondue for a rich, comforting meal.
Let's Do: Beachparty brings tropical fun. Set down a Beach Chair and a Mini Fridge, then mix a Coconut Cocktail or Honey Cocktail. Hunt for buried message-in-a-bottle loot and relax by the waves.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.g: The alluring gleam of gold isn't just for show—it also captivates creatures beneath the surface. The Golden Fishing Rod uses its special alloy to lure fish faster and boost your luck. A perfect choice for the lucky angler!
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.d: For the moments when waves crash hardest and ocean giants strain your line, you need something stronger. The Diamond Fishing Rod is forged to withstand the toughest fights and fiercest currents. With near-infinite durability, it is essential for long expeditions.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.n: The legendary Neptunium Fishing Rod, spoken of in sailor myths... This rod can conquer not only the deepest oceans but also the boiling lava seas of the Nether. Prepare to catch the magmatic mysteries below!
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.l: In the Embers of the Nether

Once your Neptunium rod is ready, leave the cool streams of the Overworld behind and brave the bubbling lava oceans of the Nether. Catch magmatic fish like the Magma Mackerel, but beware—you might burn just as easily as your catch!
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.h: The hook at the end of your line shapes your fortune. Iron hooks preserve durability, gold hooks bring luck, and double hooks offer a chance to catch two fish at once. Choose your hook wisely.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.a: Advanced hooks like Redstone or Slime hooks modify your fishing speed or catch behavior. Redstone hooks lure fish rapidly, while slime hooks make it harder for them to slip away. Power up your rod with technology!
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.n: Only the most patient anglers, attuned to the rhythm of the rivers, can pull a Neptune's Bounty box from the deep. This ancient chest holds the secrets and treasures of the sea gods themselves.
Neptunium Ingots harvested from Neptune's Bounty are made of a legendary metal that never rusts and carries the power of the ocean. Forge them into ultimate tools and armor.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.m: While fishing, the waves will occasionally wash a glass bottle onto your line. This Message in a Bottle could contain the diary of a lost sailor, a mysterious poem, or a forgotten treasure map. Break it open and listen to the whispers of the sea.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.l: Your rod should reflect your style. Craft Fishing Lines and Bobbers dyed in different colors to fully customize its appearance. Choose your color and let it float.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.n: Worms and leeches are natural delicacies that fish cannot resist. Equipping bait on your rod significantly reduces bite times. Don't forget to dig up some worms from moist soil!
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.m: Fish aren't the only things at the bottom of the water—sunken wrecks, lost armor, and iron relics lie there too. Equipping a Magnetic Bait boosts your chance to pull up treasures instead of fish.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.t: Tide's Lucky Bait is designed to coax hidden underwater secrets to the surface. With these artificial lures, multiply your luck and reel in catches that would normally remain out of reach.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.a: Deep-sea creatures like anglerfish that dwell in the pitch-black trenches don't care for ordinary bait. Abyss Bait and Incandescent Bait glow in the dark, drawing these strange creatures to your line.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.d: Complete the ensemble with the Diving Suit and Leggings. This heavy gear increases pressure resistance in deep waters and allows you to swim as swiftly as a fish.

In the dark waters where sunlight never reaches, strange bioluminescent creatures live. Dive to the deepest sea floor with your diving suit and use abyssal bait to catch rare species like the Anglerfish.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.p: Harvest shimmering Pearls from clams and oysters on the ocean floor. Use them for elegant decorations or combine them into valuable Pearl Blocks.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.s: Cook crabs and shrimp harvested from your traps to prepare delicious seafood plates. They offer high saturation and keep you energized all day long.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.g: Combine the elegance of Let's Do Candlelight with your fresh catch. Prepare Salmon on White Wine to turn your dining table into a fine restaurant. Where a master angler meets a master chef!
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.macera_basi.basic: Before you can fish, farm, or explore, you need the basics. Craft a Crafting Table — it's the foundation of everything you'll build in this world. Once you have it, a whole world of possibilities opens up!
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.macera_basi.backp: Tip: The Carry On mod lets you pick up small blocks like chests and furnaces by shift-right-clicking — combine it with your backpack for ultimate mobility!
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.macera_basi.first: The heart of this modpack is fishing! Head to the nearest river or ocean, catch your first fish, and begin your angling journey. The Aquaculture mod replaces vanilla rods with durable, upgradeable metal ones. As a reward, here's an Iron Fishing Rod and some worms to get you started. Tight lines!
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.ciftcilik_ve_gast: The First Harvest

Every great meal starts with a simple blade. Craft a Flint Knife from Farmer's Delight — it lets you harvest Straw from grass and get clean cuts from animals. The foundation of all cooking begins here.
Precision Cutting
The Cutting Board lets you slice, dice, and process ingredients with a knife. Place items on it and right-click with a knife to break them down. Essential for advanced recipes.
Bakery Apprentice
Let's Do Bakery brings the art of baking to life. Craft a Tray, learn to shape dough, and bake your first bread. Cakes, croissants, and pastries await the dedicated baker!
Reading the Seasons

With Serene Seasons, crops grow differently each season. Craft a Calendar to track the current season and plan your planting schedule. Some crops only thrive in spring, others in autumn — timing is everything.
The Corn Field
Corn Delight adds towering corn crops and hearty recipes. Plant Corn Seeds, watch them grow tall, and craft Cornbread, Popcorn, or Corn Soup. The golden grain of the plains.
Farmer's Breakfast
Let's Do: Farm \& Charm enriches farm life with new crops and meals. Grow Barley and Oats, raise Strawberries, and cook a hearty Farmer's Breakfast to start your day right.
Automated Irrigation
The Water Sprinkler from Farm \& Charm hydrates and accelerates nearby crops automatically. Place it above water and watch your fields thrive. Also try Fertilized Soil for even richer harvests.
Compact Farming

Botany Pots let you grow crops in a single block — perfect for indoor farms or space-limited builds. Place soil and a seed in the pot and watch it grow. Upgrade to Hopper Botany Pots for auto-collection.
Coffee Connoisseur
Discover coffee beans from Farmer's Respite and Croptopia. Brew a strong cup of Coffee for a powerful speed boost. Coffee Cake makes for an excellent treat alongside your brew.
The Wine Cellar

Build a proper wine production line: an Apple Press for juicing, a Fermentation Barrel for aging, and barrels for storage. Aged wines offer unique, powerful effects. Red Wine, White Wine, Apple Cider — each has its charm.
The Spice Route
Croptopia introduces a world of exotic crops. Collect Ginger, Turmeric, Basil, Coffee Beans, and Vanilla from different biomes. These spices and ingredients unlock hundreds of advanced recipes.
The Orchard

Plant fruit trees from Croptopia: Oranges, Bananas, Cherries, Lemons, and more. Right-click mature leaf blocks to harvest fruit. Craft fruit Saplings by combining two fruits with a vanilla sapling. A thriving orchard is a chef's paradise.
A Farmhouse Meal

Prepare a proper farmhouse meal. Cook Steak and Potatoes from Farmer's Delight, a Goulash from Farm \& Charm, or Ratatouille for a vegetarian option. These meals provide excellent saturation and effects.
Delightful Creations
The Delightful mod adds exotic dishes and specialty knives. Try Coconut Curry, Baklava, Matcha, or Cheeseburger. Craft an Amethyst Knife or Quartz Knife for harvesting unique materials.
The Brewery
Let's Do Brewery introduces Oktoberfest-style brewing. Craft a Wooden Brewingstation, grow Hops, and brew classic Beers and Whiskeys. Try the special craft whiskeys — each has a unique effect. Prost!
The Art of Fermentation

Brewin' And Chewin's Keg lets you ferment drinks, cheese, and specialty foods. Craft a Keg and discover Mead, Beer, Vodka, Rice Wine, and even Flaxen Cheese. Temperature matters — some recipes need the Keg to be warm or chilly.
The Grand Feast

Prepare a full Roast Chicken Feast! Feasts are multi-serving blocks that can be placed and eaten by multiple players. They provide massive nourishment and status effects. A true chef's centerpiece achievement.
A World of Flavors

Spice of Life: Carrot Edition rewards you for eating diverse foods with permanent health bonuses. Craft the Food Book to track your progress. The more unique foods you try, the more max health you gain. Aim for 30 unique foods!
The Mechanical Kitchen

Combine Create's engineering with cooking! Use Deployers to automate Cutting Boards, Mechanical Presses for shaping, and the Blazing Stove from Create Central Kitchen for boosted cooking. The ultimate automated food factory awaits.
Master Chef

You have mastered every culinary art — from humble field cooking to grand feasts, from vineyard to brewery, from spice routes to mechanical kitchens. Craft the Netherite Brewingstation as proof of your ultimate gastronomic mastery. You are the realm's greatest chef.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.aricilik_ve_dostl: Modern Beekeeping

Productive Bees turns humble vanilla bees into a long-form progression game. Hidden solitary nests are scattered across every biome; find one, place the right nest block nearby, bait it with a Honey Treat, and your first wild bee will move in. From there, the apiary you build becomes the heart of your homestead.
Sweeten the Deal

Honey Treats are the universal currency of the apiary. You need them to bait solitary nests, lure wild bees, transport bees between hives, and prime breeding chambers. Keep at least a stack in your inventory before you set out — running out mid-hunt is the fastest way to lose a rare specimen.
Industrial Apiary

The Advanced Beehive is where the magic happens. Unlike vanilla hives, no campfire is needed — resource bees calmly produce combs on their own. Each wood type has its own hive variant, so pick a biome that matches your favorite bees. Right-click a bee in JEI (U) to check its flower requirements.
Spinning the Honey

The Centrifuge converts honeycombs into bottles, wax, and bee-specific resources without destroying the combs themselves. Start with the hand-cranked version for early-game production, then graduate to the Heated and Powered models once you wire up automation.
Man's Best Friend

Doggy Talents Next rebuilds wolves into proper companions. Use Training Treats to unlock talent slots, then spend points to teach a dog to hunt, guard, herd, or even fetch items for you. A well-trained dog is one of the strongest quality-of-life tools in the modpack.
A Cozy Home

A Dog Bed marks your companion's respawn point and gives them a place to rest. Right-click with empty hands (or sneak-right-click with a bone) to claim the bed for a specific dog. If they die far from home, you can revive them by right-clicking the bed — a small luxury that pays for itself on every risky trip.
Pet Care

Domestication Innovation extends the Doggy Talents system with Pet Beds, collar upgrades, and accessory models. Set a Pet Bed as a true respawn anchor, then equip your companions with collars, shields, and magnet models to make them useful on long supply runs.
Bee Cage

Craft a Bee Cage so you can safely capture and transport wild bees between nests, hives, and breeding chambers. Sneak-right-click a bee to pick it up, then drop it near your destination. This is the very first tool that turns bee hunting into a real apiary.
Nest Locator

The Nest Locator points toward the nearest hidden solitary nest. Carry one whenever you explore a new biome — it tells you which direction to walk and which biome to search when you're hunting for a specific rare species.
Sturdy Cage

The Sturdy Bee Cage is built for rougher work. It safely carries aggressive or Nether-bred bees that would smash a normal cage. Once you start breeding lineages from hostile biomes, this is the only safe way to move them between apiaries.
Treat on a Stick

A Treat on a Stick lets you mount a tamed Bumble Bee like a small flying mount. It is both a fun early-game mobility tool and a charming reward for taming your first bee. Combine it with a saddle and Honey Treats to keep your mount happy.
Feeding Slab

The Feeding Slab centralizes flowers in one place so bees don't have to wander far to pollinate. Drop honey fluid on it to double the pollination rate. Place a slab next to your apiary and you can replace scattered flower beds with one neat block.
Bee Jar

A Bee Jar is a decorative display case for a single captured bee. Use it to show off your rarest specimens on a shelf instead of hiding them in cages or hives. Each jar is craftable in any wood colour, so it fits neatly with the rest of your builds.
Expansion Box

An Expansion Box increases the capacity of an Advanced Beehive, letting more bees share the same structure. Each box adds room for additional workers and speeds up comb production. Match the wood type of the box to the hive it attaches to.
Spruce Hive

Every wood type has its own Advanced Beehive. The Spruce variant is the next step once you want a cleaner, more specialised apiary. Spruce wood is everywhere in cold taigas, making it a convenient upgrade for early-game players.
First Wax

Wax is the first major by-product of centrifuging combs. It becomes the base material for hive upgrades, candle recipes, and the wax blocks used to build cozy honey-themed interiors. Start stockpiling it the moment your Centrifuge is online.
First Resource Comb

Resource bees turn the apiary into a true material farm — iron, copper, gold, and beyond. Produce your first configurable honeycomb to prove the line is alive. Once it drops, you can breed, centrifuge, and scale it just like any other bee product.
Sugarbag Comb

Sugarbag Bees produce a sweet comb that can be eaten directly or used as breeding material. It is a strong sign that your apiary is producing specialised strains — and a tasty snack for long foraging trips.
Blazing Comb

Blazing Bees are your first step into Nether beekeeping. Their combs fuel blaze-rod production and other hot recipes, so they belong in any serious resource pipeline. Keep them in a hot, fireproof apiary.
Bone Comb

Bone Bees convert their combs into bones and bone meal — two resources you can never have enough of. Centrifuge a batch and you'll have compost, fertiliser, and taming treats ready for the rest of the homestead.
Heated Centrifuge

The Heated Centrifuge is the first automation upgrade for comb processing. It runs on a heat source, processes much faster, and removes the last reason to keep turning the crank by hand. Pair it with a blaze farm for a steady supply of fuel.
Powered Centrifuge

The Powered Centrifuge is the fastest comb processor in the mod. It runs on redstone power, processes stacks at a time, and keeps producing wax as a bonus. This is the heart of any late-game apiary production line.
Bottler
The Bottler automatically fills empty glass bottles with honey fluid. Once your apiary produces more honey than you can drink, set one up and pipe the bottles into your kitchen, market, or storage.
Catcher

The Catcher automatically packs bees leaving a hive into cages. It's one of the cleanest ways to automate transfer and sorting in a multi-apiary base. Combine it with Item Conduits or hoppers to route bees where they need to go.
Honey Generator

The Honey Generator turns honey fluid into Forge Energy. At this point the apiary can power the rest of your base on its own — every bottle you bottle or honeycomb you centrifuge can be rerouted here for a self-sustaining energy loop.
Breeding Chamber

The Breeding Chamber is the controlled way to combine two bees into a new strain. Place your selected bees inside, watch their cooldown, and check JEI for the offspring possibilities. This is where planning starts to matter more than luck.
BaBee Incubator

The Incubator speeds up baby bee growth by feeding them honey treats. With one online, every breeding cycle finishes in seconds instead of minutes — a small upgrade that pays off hundreds of times across a full playthrough.
Gene Indexer

The Gene Indexer stores and organises the gene samples you collect. Send it a redstone pulse to combine genes into their 100% variants — these are the building blocks of every perfect bee you will ever breed.
Gene Sample

A Gene Sample captures a bee's traits — productivity, endurance, behaviour — for later use in breeding. Right-click any bee to harvest a sample. The sample's tier dictates how likely it is to be inherited by an offspring.
Squashed Bee Material

Squashed Bee Material is the dense, advanced form of bee genetics. Centrifuge a bottle to extract all six gene types at once, then feed them back into your best bees. It belongs in high-tier breeding recipes and serious late-game research.
Upgrade Base

The Upgrade Base is the foundation for every hive upgrade. Craft one for each Advanced Beehive and you can then attach Productivity, Speed, Range, Biome, Simulator, Filter, or Gene Sampler upgrades to customise the hive's behaviour.
Productivity Alpha
Productivity Alpha is the first output boost for a hive. It's the moment your apiary starts producing more than a single bee could manage alone. Pair it with Speed to compound the effect.
Productivity Omega

Productivity Omega crowns the productivity line after Alpha, Beta, and Gamma. It is expensive, but it pushes a single hive to its absolute peak. Combine it with Speed and Range upgrades for absurd output numbers.
Speed Upgrade
The Speed Upgrade shortens the hive cycle so combs arrive more often. It is strongest when paired with Productivity and Simulator upgrades, turning a slow resource bee into a non-stop producer.
Simulator Upgrade
The Simulator Upgrade lets a hive work without sending bees out to find flowers. It removes flower placement from the equation entirely, which is one of the key pieces of any fully-automated apiary.
Range Upgrade

The Range Upgrade expands the working area of bees that interact with nearby blocks — Farmer, Rancher, Collector, Lumber. It helps when one setup needs to cover a larger farm, orchard, or tree plantation.
Bee Bomb

A Bee Bomb floods the area with bees when thrown. It's part joke, part utility — useful when you need to populate an empty hive fast, or when you simply want to prank a friend. The aggravating variant enrages the swarm.
Inactive Dragon Egg

The Inactive Dragon Egg is the setup item for end-game bee breeding. Activate it with Dragon Breath and you unlock one of the mod's most exotic lines. Treat it with care — only one egg can exist in your inventory at a time.
Dragon Egg Hive

The Dragon Egg Hive is the special home for an activated dragon egg. It is the gateway to the strongest and rarest bee products in the chapter — the rewards here are measured in elytras and netherite.
Draconic Chunk

Draconic Chunk is the final proof that your apiary reached the top end of progression. If you can make this, your bee line has truly paid off. Reward on completion: an Elytra, netherite, and 24 honey treats to celebrate.
Productivity Beta

Productivity Beta is the second step in the output line. Combine it with Alpha and Gamma upgrades and your bees start producing noticeable, scalable surpluses. It's the upgrade that tells you the apiary is ready for serious production.
Productivity Gamma
Productivity Gamma bridges the gap between midgame bee farms and the final Omega tier. Layer it with Speed and Range upgrades to get real, measurable throughput numbers in your Centrifuge logs.
BaBee Upgrade

The BaBee Upgrade gives a chance for a new baby bee to be spawned from the hive every time honey is delivered. The bee type depends on which bees are present in the hive. It does not work with bees that can't self-breed. Multiple upgrades can be installed for a greater chance, and in a Catcher only baby bees will be caught.
Gene Sampling
The Bee Sampler upgrade slowly pulls gene samples from visiting bees. It turns every working hive into a passive genetics lab — come back later to find a stockpile of rare gene samples waiting.
Super Treat

Doggy Talents' Super Treat is the upgraded version of the basic training treat. Use it to push a wolf companion past the early talent cap and into the late-game tier — extra levels, extra abilities, extra loyalty.
Magnet Model

The Magnet model gives pets a handy pickup effect for loose items on the ground. It's a small quality-of-life upgrade that pays off during long mining trips, supply runs, or battles in wide open biomes.
Floral Trail

Every bee colony starts with flowers. Pick a poppy from the nearest meadow — poppies are easy to find in plains and flower forests, and many productive bees use them as a basic pollen source. Once you have flowers in hand, the rest of the apiary chain becomes possible.
Buttercup Bloom

The Buttercup is a Buzzier Bees specialty flower that only spawns in flower forests and meadows. It's a popular pollen source for yellow-loving bees and a quick way to add a splash of colour to your garden. Plant one near a Feeding Slab for a permanent supply.
White Clover Carpet

White Clover is a small ground-cover flower from Buzzier Bees. Several productive bee species use it as their primary pollen source, so a patch of clover next to your apiary will keep a swarm busy without any further effort on your part.
First Honeycomb

The vanilla honeycomb is the first material that lets you reach the apiary. Find a vanilla bee nest in an oak or birch forest, harvest a few combs with shears, and you have the base ingredient for Bee Cages, Bottler inputs, and the first generation of useful items.
Nether Brick Nest

Nether Brick Nests can only be settled inside Nether fortresses — they attract blaze-themed bees that produce Blazing Combs. Build a small brick enclosure in the Nether, bait it with Honey Treats, and you have a steady supply of blaze-related resources without ever fighting a blaze again.
Soul Sand Nest

Soul Sand Nests are placed in soul sand valleys to capture ghostly bees. Their combs centrifuge into Soul Sand and Spectral materials — useful for any build or recipe that needs a touch of the Nether's quieter biomes.
End Stone Nest

End Stone Nests can only be settled in the End's outer islands. They attract Ender-themed bees that produce rare End Stone and chorus-fruit related resources. Bring plenty of Honey Treats, an Elytra, and patience — the End is far from a casual trip.
Slime Nest

Slime Nests go in slime chunks underground. The bees that settle there produce Slime Balls and related sticky materials — a renewable alternative to hunting slimes directly. Build one near your mob farm and let the bees do the work.
Honey Apple

The Honey Apple is a quick snack crafted from apples and honey bottles. It's a low-tier food item, but it stacks easily, restores hunger fast, and gives you an excuse to use the honey you no longer need for Bee Cages. Keep a stack in your hotbar for long foraging trips.
Honey Bread

Honey Bread is a step up from plain bread — it restores more hunger and can be mass-produced once your Bottler is online. Use it as a mid-game staple food while you wait for the late-game gourmet recipes to unlock.
Glazed Porkchop

Honey-Glazed Porkchop is a late-game dish that combines cooked pork with honey for a powerful food item. It's expensive to make, but it stacks with the Spice of Life bonus and is perfect for boss fights or long mining expeditions.
Bottle of Bee

A Bottle of Bee is a decorative item from Buzzier Bees that lets you carry a single bee in a glass bottle. Use it as a portable capture option, or display it as a trophy on a shelf. Throw it to release the bee safely when you need it again.
Moobloom Friend

The Moobloom is a flower-covered cow from Buzzier Bees. It wanders your pasture, leaves flowers in its wake, and occasionally drops honeyed materials. It's a decorative mob but also a small pollinator that keeps your apiary fed when you run out of planted flowers.
Grizzly Bear Companion

The Grizzly Bear is a tamable mount from Buzzier Bees. It can be saddled and ridden like a horse, and it packs a serious melee punch. Combined with the rest of your companion system, the bear fills the role of heavy combat escort while you explore.
Wax Block

A Wax Block is nine wax pieces compressed into a single build block. Use it as a clean storage unit — one block holds nine wax, which is the perfect amount for crafting upgrade bases in bulk. It's also a sweet decorative block that fits honey-themed interiors.
Hive Architecture: Bricks

Buzzier Bees adds Honeycomb Bricks, Tiles, Doors, and Trapdoors — a full decorative set themed around the apiary. Build a honey-coloured house, a chapel, or just a storage room that fits the rest of your homestead's theme.
Honey Lamp

The Honey Lamp is a soft, warm light source from Buzzier Bees. Place a row above your workbench or hang them from your apiary ceiling — they cost less than glowstone and give off a more honey-toned glow.
Crystalline Flower

The Crystalline Flower grows only in The Bumblezone dimension — bring a stack of Honey Treats and use them as currency to trade with the local bees. It pollinates almost every rare productive bee species, so a steady supply unlocks strains you can't get in the Overworld.
Royal Jelly Reserve

Royal Jelly Blocks are valuable crafting materials in The Bumblezone's economy. Trade Honey Treats for them with the dimension's bee NPCs, then bring them home as emergency fuel for high-tier breeding recipes or for unique potions.
Honey Crystal

Honey Crystals are fragile but powerful light sources from The Bumblezone. They drop a small amount of luck when mined, and they're a required ingredient in a few late-game Bumblezone recipes. Pick one up and bring it home the next time you visit.
Glistering Crystal

The Glistering Honey Crystal is a rare variant brewed from a Honey Crystal and a special Bumblezone ingredient. It gives off a powerful glow and is used to brew the Potion of Luck. Build up a stockpile before any major mining expedition.
Filter Upgrade

The Filter Upgrade sits on top of an Upgrade Base and lets you restrict a hive to specific bees, specific flowers, or both. Use it to keep one rogue bee from polluting a carefully-bred lineage or to prevent bees from wandering into the wrong biome.
Comb Block Upgrade

The Comb Block Upgrade converts harvested combs directly into Comb Blocks instead of loose pieces. One block holds nine combs in a single stack — ideal for compact storage and long-distance transport via Create, item ducts, or shulker boxes.
Anti-Teleport Upgrade

The Anti-Teleport Upgrade prevents bees inside the hive from being teleported. Critical for apiaries in the End where chorus-fruit teleports can scatter an entire population in seconds. Pair it with a Simulator for a fully hands-off End apiary.
Whistle Commands

The Whistle broadcasts commands to all your following dogs at once — sit, heel, stand, go to bed, howl, even carry you on a Wolf Mount. Sneak-right-click to cycle between modes. With one in your hotbar, you direct your entire pack without typing anything.
Canine Tracker

The Canine Tracker is a compass-style item that points toward a specific dog you've previously registered with it. Right-click a dog to bind the tracker to it, then check the direction whenever you can't see your companion on the map.
Locator Orb

Locator Orbs are collectable trophies from Doggy Talents — twelve themed orbs that unlock unique cosmetic effects for your dog. The basic Dyable variant lets you recolour it to match any collar or build palette you use across the modpack.
Treat Bag

The Treat Bag stores up to a stack of dog treats and lets you throw them at distance. Sneak-right-click to open the bag and refill it. It's the cleanest way to keep your dogs fed while mining, exploring, or fighting — no more dropped items littering the floor.
Throw Stick

Throw Sticks are toys your dogs will chase and bring back. Each retrieved stick trains a small amount of XP and reinforces the Fetch talent. Keep one in your off-hand for endless entertainment while waiting for a Centrifuge to finish its cycle.
Frisbee

Frisbees behave like throw sticks but travel farther and reward a different talent. Pair them with the Fetch talent for compound training. The Drool Frisbee variant trains faster but slobbers all over your inventory — bring wipes.
Rice Farming

Rice Grains are a Doggy Talents food crop that grows near water. Plant a flooded field and you'll have a renewable source of dog food independent of meat drops. The Rice Bowl is a comfort food that heals dogs faster than raw rice.
Rice Mill

The Rice Mill converts harvested rice into dehusked grains ready for cooking. Combine it with bowls for full Rice Bowls, or feed the grains directly as dog food. A single mill can process a full field's harvest in seconds.
Deflection Shield

The Deflection Shield is a collar accessory that periodically deflects a portion of incoming damage away from your pet. It's a single crafted item but lasts a long time and stacks with other collar models. Equip it on your most exposed companion.
Feather on a Stick

The Feather on a Stick is a fun pet toy — chase your cat or dog around with it to trigger play animations and a small amount of XP. It's not load-bearing, but it makes your companion feel like a real animal instead of just a combat pet.
Wayward Lantern

The Wayward Lantern is a soul-lantern style light source from Domestication Innovation. Place it at your pet's respawn bed so the bed is easy to find at night, or use it as a soft warm light source in your house without a glowstone budget.
Pet Bed Collection

Domestication Innovation ships Pet Beds in every wool colour. Build at least one of each — it costs almost nothing once you have wool from your sheep farm, and a full row of beds turns a side room into a real sanctuary for your entire companion pack.
Draconic Dust

Draconic Dust is the powdered form of Draconic Chunks, dropped during the endgame dragon-bee line. Use it to brew Potion of Luck at industrial scale or as a catalyst in the highest-tier Productive Bees recipes.
Amber Block

Amber Blocks are decorative, glowing blocks produced by amber-themed bees. They fit perfectly into a Bumblezone-themed base or any cozy honey-coloured interior. Combine with Honey Lamp and Crystalline Flowers for a stunning room.
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.n: Neptunium metal holds magical subaquatic properties. Crafting a full set of Neptunium gear allows you to breathe underwater, swim with incredible speed, and move unrestricted on the sea floor. Forge a piece of Neptunium armor or tools to claim this power!
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.m: Oceans are not just filled with marine life, but also with historical debris and sunken treasures. Using magnetic bait and lucky hooks, try to salvage some historical junk or metal items from the sea floor. Retrieve some Driftwood or a Gold Ring from the water!
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.a: Manually cutting fish slices becomes exhausting when you have nets and traps full of them. Use Kinetically powered slicing machines to automate your seafood production! Craft a Slicer from Slice & Dice to process your catch effortlessly.

The Abyssal Chasm is the deepest, darkest ocean biome in the world, where the pressure is immense and giant sea monsters roam. Normal diving gear won't protect you here. You need to build a heavy Submarine to explore this luminescent biome safely. Find some Sea Glass Shards to prove your success!
Heavy Diving Suit of the Abyss

To explore the Abyssal Chasm on foot without a submarine, you need to forge the ultimate heavy diving armor using materials found in the deep. This armor protects you from the crushing deep-sea pressure and lets you walk freely in the dark. Craft the Diving Helmet of the Abyss!
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.s: A true master angler knows that seasons shift the currents and temperature, bringing different species. Catching a tropical Angelfish in warm summer waters and a chorus-infused Chorus Cod in the coldest conditions will prove your mastery over the seasons!
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.t: Combine the delicacies of Let's Do Candlelight with the exotic fish of the deep. Cook a Tropical Fish Supreme using cod slices, onion, mushrooms, and milk, and roll some Deep Sea Sushi from the Abyssal Chasm. A feast fit for a king!
kubejs.ftbquests.quest.baliki_ve_deniz.b: After all the dangerous deep-sea dives and freezing winter catches, it's time to enjoy the warm sunshine. Craft a beach chair, open some coconuts, mix a Coconut Cocktail and a Honey Cocktail, and throw a relaxing beach party! You've earned it, Captain!
